# Paolo Donà

> Software engineer turned executive. Chief Information Officer at Staycity Group, Europe's leading aparthotel operator. Keenly interested in AI, automation, and building small, fast-paced, world-class technical teams.

Personal website of Paolo Donà (https://paolodona.com). The site is static HTML, with Markdown mirrors and a structured JSON corpus for AI agents. Canonical structured data uses Schema.org JSON-LD and references Paolo as https://paolodona.com/#paolo.

## Core Pages

- [About](https://paolodona.com/index.html.md): Bio, current role, interests, and contact links.
- [Work](https://paolodona.com/work.html.md): Career history across software engineering, startups, and C-level technology leadership.
- [Side Projects](https://paolodona.com/side-projects.html.md): Whatevernote, Este Bike, and other projects built outside the day job.
- [Bookshelf](https://paolodona.com/bookshelf.html.md): Books Paolo Donà is reading or has read and enjoyed.
- [Blog](https://paolodona.com/blog.html.md): Notes and writing on technology, leadership, AI, and life.

## Structured Data

- [Machine-readable site corpus](https://paolodona.com/site.json): Profile, work history, education, side projects, books, posts, feeds, and canonical URLs as JSON.
- [Full LLM corpus](https://paolodona.com/llms-full.txt): Full Markdown text of the public site and blog posts.
- [Sitemap](https://paolodona.com/sitemap.xml): XML sitemap for crawl discovery.
- [RSS feed](https://paolodona.com/feed.xml): Blog feed.

## Blog Posts

- [Cycling King Alfred's Way for Focus Ireland and Crisis](https://paolodona.com/blog/king-alfreds-way-charity-cycle.html.md): 24th May 2026. In June I will ride King Alfred's Way with Staycity colleagues in memory of Keith Freeman, raising funds for Focus Ireland and Crisis.
- [Talking to Oracle OPERA in plain English](https://paolodona.com/blog/opera-mcp.html.md): 1st May 2026. I built an MCP server that puts Oracle OPERA Cloud — one of the most quintessentially "old-style enterprise" systems in hospitality — behind a Claude prompt. Here is what changed when our team stopped clicking and started asking.
- [Speaking at Stripe Sessions 2026 in San Francisco](https://paolodona.com/blog/stripe-sessions-2026.html.md): 30th April 2026. Sharing the blueprint we used at Staycity Group to unify our multi-channel payment infrastructure on stage at Stripe Sessions 2026 in San Francisco.
- [Borrowing expertise: a 30-property Booking.com merchandising audit](https://paolodona.com/blog/merchandising-audit.html.md): 27th February 2026. I am not a revenue manager and I am not a copywriter. Over a long weekend, with Claude Code as a collaborator, I produced a 30-property Booking.com merchandising audit grounded in pricing theory, behavioural psychology and a real side-by-side comparison against competitors. A note on what changes when domain knowledge stops being the bottleneck.

- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/paolodona
- Work email: paolo.dona@staycity.com
- Personal email: paolo.dona@gmail.com

- [Human-readable home page](https://paolodona.com/): Canonical visual home page.
